description: Key terms, field names, and domain concepts used across the openFDA REST APIs

terms.label: FDA Adverse Event Reporting System
terms.definition: A database containing adverse event reports, medication error reports, and product quality complaints submitted to FDA for drugs and therapeutic biological products.

terms.label: CFSAN Adverse Event Reporting System
terms.definition: A database supporting the FDA Center for Food Safety and Applied Nutrition (CFSAN) post-marketing safety surveillance program for adverse events related to foods, dietary supplements, and cosmetics.

terms.label: Structured Product Labeling
terms.definition: A document markup standard for the exchange of information about a regulated product using XML and controlled vocabulary. FDA uses SPL for prescription and over-the-counter drug product labeling.

terms.label: Unique Ingredient Identifier
terms.definition: A non-proprietary, free, unique, unambiguous, non-semantic, alphanumeric identifier based on a substance's molecular structure and/or descriptive information.

terms.label: Global Unique Device Identification Database
terms.definition: The FDA database that serves as the authoritative source of information on device identifiers (DI portion of the UDI).

terms.label: Unique Device Identifier
terms.definition: A unique numeric or alphanumeric code that consists of two components: a device identifier (DI) and a production identifier (PI).

terms.label: Premarket Notification 510(k)
terms.definition: A premarket submission made to FDA to demonstrate that the device to be marketed is at least as safe and effective as a legally marketed device not subject to PMA.

terms.label: Premarket Approval
terms.definition: The FDA process of scientific and regulatory review to evaluate the safety and effectiveness of Class III medical devices.

terms.label: National Drug Code
terms.definition: A unique 10-digit, 3-segment number that identifies the labeler, product, and trade package size of drug products.

terms.label: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities
terms.definition: A medical terminology used to classify adverse event information associated with the use of biopharmaceuticals and other medical products.

terms.label: openFDA Harmonization Fields
terms.definition: A field within openFDA API results that contains harmonized data drawn from FDA systems (e.g., NDC, brand name, generic name, application number).

terms.label: Response Metadata
terms.definition: A top-level object in every openFDA API response containing disclaimer, terms, license, last_updated, and pagination results information.

terms.label: Search Parameter
terms.definition: The query parameter used in openFDA API requests to filter results using Lucene query syntax (e.g., search=patient.drug.medicinalproduct:ASPIRIN).

terms.label: Count Parameter
terms.definition: The query parameter used to return aggregated counts of a specific field across all records matching the search criteria.

terms.label: Limit Parameter
terms.definition: The query parameter to control the number of records returned per page (max 1000, default 1).

terms.label: Skip Parameter
terms.definition: The query parameter to skip a number of records for pagination (max 25000).

terms.label: Safety Report ID
terms.definition: A unique identifier for an adverse event safety report submitted to FAERS.

terms.label: Seriousness
terms.definition: An indicator in FAERS reports denoting whether the adverse event was serious (e.g., resulted in death, hospitalization, or disability).

terms.label: Recall Number
terms.definition: A unique identifier assigned by FDA to a specific recall action.

terms.label: Recall Classification
terms.definition: The FDA's classification of a recall based on risk: Class I (most serious), Class II (moderate risk), or Class III (least serious).

terms.label: Voluntary or Mandated Recall
terms.definition: Indicates whether a recall was initiated voluntarily by the firm or mandated by FDA.

terms.label: Device Event Type
terms.definition: Categorizes the medical device adverse event as Death, Injury, Malfunction, or Other.

terms.label: Reaction MedDRA Preferred Term
terms.definition: The MedDRA preferred term describing the adverse reaction reported in a FAERS case.

terms.label: Drug Indication
terms.definition: The indication for use of the drug as reported in an adverse event report.

terms.label: Brand Name
terms.definition: The trade or proprietary name of the drug product as found in the openFDA harmonization block.

terms.label: Generic Name
terms.definition: The established, non-proprietary name of the drug as found in the openFDA harmonization block.

terms.label: Route of Administration
terms.definition: The path by which a drug is taken into the body (e.g., oral, intravenous, topical).

terms.label: Product Code
terms.definition: A three-letter code assigned by FDA that identifies the generic category of a device.

terms.label: Device Class
terms.definition: The regulatory class assigned to a medical device based on risk: Class I, II, or III.
